title: Jack Black & Paul Rudd's Anaconda Reboot Sets Franchise's Second-Biggest Box Office Debut
text: Jack Black and Paul Rudd's 2025 Anaconda reboot achieved strong box office numbers during its December debut. The film earned between $12.9 million and $14.4 million for its three-day opening weekend, positioning it as the second-best opening weekend of the franchise behind the original 1997 film. The original Anaconda (1997) had a $16.6 million domestic debut and $137 million worldwide. Anacondas: The Hunt for the Blood Orchid (2004) had a $12.8 million domestic debut and $71 million worldwide. The 2025 Anaconda opening was $12.9-$14.4 million with final totals pending. Despite the modest opening, industry analysts note the film benefits from its holiday season release timing. Unlike previous theatrical entries, this meta-comedy about filmmakers who encounter a real giant snake can expect slow and steady growth rather than frontloaded opening weekends, potentially allowing it to outperform earlier franchise entries in total gross. The $45 million budget means the film needs substantial worldwide earnings to achieve profitability.
